# Microsoft OneDrive Connector - Full Documentation

# Microsoft OneDrive Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Microsoft OneDrive (OneDrive Connector) enables you to access Microsoft Graph
APIs to provide a unified programmability model that you can use to access the tremendous amount of
data in Microsoft 365, Windows 10, and Enterprise Mobility + Security.

For compatibility information and fixed issues, see the Microsoft OneDrive Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- Microsoft Graph API 1.0
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the target resource
- Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

Some common use cases for OneDrive Connector include:

- Sales Operations
  - When a sales contract comes in through the Salesforce CRM system, upload the contract to
    OneDrive and collaboratively review the contract with the Deal Desk. You can assign approval
    tasks to notify the contract owner when a contract is approved or rejected.
  - When a new budget proposal or customer invoice is generated, upload the documents to OneDrive
    and collaborate with the accounting team for their review. You can assign tasks to notify the
    invoice owner when an invoice is approved or rejected.
  - When an opportunity is closed (won), create a folder structure in OneDrive based on a predefined
    template.

- Employee onboarding and offboarding
  - When new employees start their jobs, existing resources can be shared with them via OneDrive.
  - Create a new folder in OneDrive and share it with the new employee so they can upload documents
    to it.
  - After an employee leaves the company, all documents used or uploaded can be moved or deleted
    (GDPR).

- Health care  
  Share sensitive patient data with the departments that are allowed to access the information by
  using OneDrive to share the files, and allow access to the relevant parties.

## Authentication Types

OneDrive Connector uses the following authentication methods:

- OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant connection type, which is used in applications that are
  installed on a device to gain access to protected resources.
- OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant, which uses the identity of the application to access
  web-hosted resources.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Microsoft OneDrive Connector Reference](microsoft-onedrive-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Microsoft OneDrive Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Microsoft OneDrive (OneDrive Connector) enables you to access Microsoft Graph
APIs.

## Microsoft OneDrive Config

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#OnedriveConfig_OauthAuthorizationCode">OAuth v2.0 Authorization Code</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#OnedriveConfig_OauthClientCredentials">OAuth v2.0 Client Credentials</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID to use across all operations unless overwritten by the operation parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifier of the element used by other components for reference.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time in milliseconds that the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout Time Unit</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection Types

#### OAuth v2.0 Authorization Code

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

##### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL for Microsoft Graph endpoints to access from the connector and from which all other endpoints are derived.</p></td><td><p><code>https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the method to use to send the resulting token back to your app. This must be <code>query</code>.</p></td><td><p>query</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumerKey as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumerSecret as registered with the service provider</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs authorization endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs accessToken endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, it defaults to the OAuth scopes in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The resource owner ID for each component to use if it is not otherwise referenced.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after an access token is received.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth v2.0 Client Credentials

Access web-hosted resources by using the identity of the application.

##### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL for Microsoft Graph endpoints to access from the connector and from which all other endpoints are derived.</p></td><td><p><code>https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth client secret as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, it defaults to the OAuth scopes in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Add Permission](#AddPermission)
- [Copy Drive Item](#CopyDriveItem)
- [Create Drive Item](#CreateDriveItem)
- [Create Sharing Link](#CreateSharingLink)
- [Delete Drive Item](#DeleteDriveItem)
- [Get Drive Item By Id](#GetDriveItemById)
- [Get Drive Item By Path](#GetDriveItemByPath)
- [Get Root](#GetRoot)
- [List Children](#ListChildren)
- [List Permissions](#ListPermissions)
- [Monitor Request](#MonitorRequest)
- [Move Drive Item](#MoveDriveItem)
- [Remove Permission](#RemovePermission)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)
- [Upload Content](#UploadContent)

## Add Permission

`<microsoftOneDrive:add-permission>`

Gives read or write access to a specified file for users that are specified in the **Permissions
Info** field.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the drive driveItem to which to add this permission.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Permissions Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Information about the permission to add.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve a list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## Copy Drive Item

`<microsoftOneDrive:copy-drive-item>`

Executes a copy request to Microsoft OneDrive.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the driveItem to copy.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parent DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the folder to copy the selected driveItem to. If unspecified, the selected file is copied to the same folder in which it resides.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Conflict name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>In case of conflict, specifies a new name for the drive item after the copy operation is completed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the Read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve the list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## Create Drive Item

`<microsoftOneDrive:create-drive-item>`

Creates a new driveItem entity type in the specified OneDrive.

### Parameters

## <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parent DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the folder to copy the selected driveItem to.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Item name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name to use when creating the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>FOLDER</p></li><li><p>FILE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The type of driveItem to create, which can be either FILE or FOLDER</p></td><td><p>FILE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Conflict behavior</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RENAME</p></li><li><p>REPLACE</p></li><li><p>FAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies what happens in case of a name conflict. Possible values are: FAIL, RENAME, REPLACE</p></td><td><p>FAIL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Item properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Additional properties to add to the file.</p></div><div><p>Use the <strong>Item properties</strong> field for <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/folder?view=graph-rest-1.0">folder</a> or <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/file?view=graph-rest-1.0">file</a> metadata settings. The following example shows the <strong>Item properties</strong> field for folder metadata settings:</p></div><div><div><pre>output application/json

{ "childCount": 1024, "view": { "@odata.type": "microsoft.graph.folderView" }
}</pre></div></div><div><p>The result request to Microsoft OneDrive is:</p></div><div><div><pre>{
"name":"New Folder", "folder": { "childCount": 1024,
"view":{"@odata.type":"microsoft.graph.folderView"} }, "@microsoft.graph.conflictBehavior":"rename"
}</pre></div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read
Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to
override the read timeout values defined in the connector
configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time
Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one
of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The
time unit value used by the read timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config
Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the
configuration to use to execute this
component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming
Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory
Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store
Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how
Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable
streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration,
one
of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location
from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity
ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to
retrieve the list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is
either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter
of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive
ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a
driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target
Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores
the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target
Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the
operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>
field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection
Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect
Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity
errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## Create Sharing Link

`<microsoftOneDrive:create-sharing-link>`

Creates a new sharing link. If a sharing link of the specified type already exists for the app, the
existing sharing link is returned.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the driveItem for which to create the sharing link.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Link Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>VIEW</p></li><li><p>EDIT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Type of sharing link to create. Accepted values are <code>view</code> or <code>edit</code>. A value of <code>view</code> creates a read-only link to the driveItem. A value of <code>edit</code> creates a read-write link to the driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scope Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ANONYMOUS</p></li><li><p>ORGANIZATION</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the link to create. Accepted values are <code>anonymous</code> and <code>organization</code>. A value of <code>anonymous</code> indicates the link is usable by anyone. A value of <code>organization</code> indicates the link is only usable for users who are signed in to the same tenant.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Date Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates the expiration time of the permission, specified as a string with a format of <code>yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ</code> for DateTime.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve a list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## Delete Drive Item

`<microsoftOneDrive:delete-drive-item>`

Deletes a driveItem based on the specified ID.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the driveItem to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve a list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## Get Drive Item By Id

`<microsoftOneDrive:get-drive-item-by-id>`

Retrieves information about the a driveItem based on the specified ID.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the driveItem to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to retrieve in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response includes information about the specified relationship.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional OData queries that are not exposed as specific parameters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve the list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## Get Drive Item By Path

`<microsoftOneDrive:get-drive-item-by-path>`

Retrieves information about a driveItem based on the specified path.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the driveItem to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to retrieve in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response will include information about the specified relationship.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional OData queries that are not exposed as specific parameters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve the list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## Get Root

`<microsoftOneDrive:get-root>`

Retrieves information about the root folder of a drive.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to retrieve in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response will include information about the specified relationship.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional OData queries that are not exposed as specific parameters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve the list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## List Children

`<microsoftOneDrive:list-children>`

Retrieves a collection of driveItems from the driveItemâs child relationships.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the folder from which to extract the list of children.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to retrieve in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response will include information about the specified relationship.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order By</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter used to specify the sort order of the items returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional OData queries that are not exposed as specific parameters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve the list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TIMEOUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED

## List Permissions

`<microsoftOneDrive:list-permissions>`

Retrieves a collection of permissions resources on a driveItem.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DriveItem ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to retrieve in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional OData queries that are not exposed as specific parameters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GROUP</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Location from which to retrieve the list of drive IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the entity to use to retrieve the list of drives.</p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <strong>Entity ID</strong> is either the user ID or the group ID, depending on which fetch location is used as the first parameter of the DriveInfo parameter group.</div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Drive ID used to locate a driveItem.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Microsoft OneDrive Config](#OnedriveConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONFLICT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:INVALID_INPUT
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:LOCKED
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTONEDRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
